evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: uses wss://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/text-to-speech/{voice_id}/stream-input with xi-api-key header, model_id eleven_flash_v2_5, base64 audio field decode — all match the known ElevenLabs realtime TTS contract. No version date stated but method names are consistent across backend and client.
evidence.executability: Backend uses websockets.connect(extra_headers=...) which works on websockets<14 but was renamed to additional_headers in v14+; pip install pins no version. Client expects PCM but no output_format is sent in the initial message to ElevenLabs (which requires a BOS message with voice_settings/output_format before text), so audio format will default and may not match the client's 44100 assumption. AudioWorklet served from /public is standard CRA. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo).
evidence.completeness: (a) API key acquisition mentioned but no explicit URL/menu path to obtain ELEVEN_API_KEY from elevenlabs.io. (b) install bash blocks present. (c) .env template present (ELEVEN_API_KEY, ELEVEN_BASE). (d) backend main.py complete. (e) frontend TTSPlayer + worklet complete. (f) test command shown as pytest snippet but no explicit single run command. Env vars listed match those referenced.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Official elevenlabs.io/docs URLs cited inline near relevant code (header auth, audio formats). Mix of official (FastAPI, MDN, ElevenLabs) and third-party (Strapi blog, SmashingMagazine, StackOverflow, GitHub issues). Official docs present and placed near code, but heavy reliance on third-party links.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; applied long-tail handling. Internally consistent, version not explicitly dated. Potential extra_headers vs additional_headers gotcha on websockets v14+ could cause TypeError at runtime depending on installed version.
